chapter_context.summary: Solomon begins construction on Mount Moriah; detailed accounts of the Holy Place, Holy of Holies (Most Holy House), golden cherubim, and the bronze pillars.

outline.text: Construction Commenced at Mount Moriah (Ornan's Threshing Floor)
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Construction Commenced at Mount Moriah (Ornan's Threshing Floor) (3:1-2), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Dimensions, Porch, and Extensive Gold Overlay of the Holy Place
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Dimensions, Porch, and Extensive Gold Overlay of the Holy Place (3:3-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: The Holy of Holies (Most Holy House), Gold Cherubim, and Veil
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Holy of Holies (Most Holy House), Gold Cherubim, and Veil (3:8-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: The Two Large Bronze Pillars (Jachin and Boaz) Erected
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Two Large Bronze Pillars (Jachin and Boaz) Erected (3:15-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: The Temple Centered Kingdom
key_themes.description: The presence of God's glory in the Temple is the absolute center of national blessing and life.

key_themes.title: Immediate Retribution and Reform
key_themes.description: Good kings who seek the LORD experience immediate victory and peace, while unfaithfulness leads to immediate warning and exile.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 3 prompt you to humble yourself, seek God's face, and turn away from compromise?
What practical reforms can you make in your daily walk to ensure your heart is dedicated to God?

cultural_historical.title: The Levitical Worship System
cultural_historical.text: Unlike the Book of Kings, Chronicles highlights the roles of singers, Levites, and liturgical musicians in the temple's daily administration.
